accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verifiable context: NPS/Interior rule, 90 Fed. Reg. 2621, CRA under chapter 8 of title 5, and enactment date are all correct.

no_hallucinations.why: Added context (CRA governs congressional review of agency rulemaking) is accurate and nothing is fabricated.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose: congressional disapproval nullifying the NPS Glen Canyon motor vehicles rule.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Describes the CRA mechanism and that the rule is stripped of force or effect under chapter 8 of title 5.

exceptions_conditions.why: The resolution contains no material exceptions or conditions, so none need be summarized.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the May 23, 2025 enactment and that there are no future deadlines; passage dates are accurately stated.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative effect—disapproval and nullification of the named rule.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and contains no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized, internally consistent, and reads clearly.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately compact with no significant padding for a one-sentence resolution.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the National Park Service, Department of the Interior, the rule title, and the CRA.

correct_figures.why: Federal Register cite (90 Fed. Reg. 2621), enactment date, and House passage date are correct; Senate May 8 passage matches the record.
